notes: This batch treats run_ultra_axis0_ab_axis6_sweep.py as the final raw-order simpy family in the current sims strip.
ultra4 was read only as the preceding full-stack comparison anchor.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Catalogs ultra4_full_stack and ultra_axis0_ab_axis6_sweep as the final adjacent ultra families.
Repo-held top-level evidence pack was searched for ultra4 and ultra sweep SIM_IDs and no matches were found.
Preceding full-stack anchor showing the current sweep is a narrowing rather than the same family.
Confirms shared keysets but materially different Stage16 and Axis0 AB values.

runtime_expectations: run_ultra_axis0_ab_axis6_sweep.py writes one results_ultra_axis0_ab_axis6_sweep.json and one local evidence block for S_SIM_ULTRA_AXIS0_AB_STAGE16_AXIS6_SWEEP.
The runner uses 8 seeds, 2048 Stage16 states, 256 Axis0 trials, 64 Axis0 cycles, 4 sequences, 2 entanglers, and 4 mix modes.
No later raw-order simpy family remains after this file in the current sims strip.
failure_modes: The final ultra sweep family can be mistaken for a repo-top evidenced surface because it has a local evidence writer and catalog admission.
The axis0_ab branch can be over-read as one uniform record contract even though it mixes absolute and delta records.
The ultra4 comparison anchor can be mistaken for the same family because the stage16 and axis0 keysets match.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
